title: Unwindulax (1)

jokes.text: Just like your mom works that street corner. I'm sorry, that's too much. No sweat. My mom is a prostitute.
jokes.setup: Standard 'your mom' insult setup
jokes.punchline: Character casually confirms the insult is literally true
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation by having the target embrace the insult rather than be offended. Clean structure and good economy.

jokes.text: Hey! New drinking game! Drink when someone says something! Whoo!
jokes.setup: Announcing a drinking game with rules
jokes.punchline: The rule is so broad it's meaningless - drink when anyone speaks
jokes.explanation: Captures the mindless party culture perfectly with an absurdly simple rule that defeats the purpose of a 'game.'

jokes.text: ♪ I caught crabs in paradise ♪ and, yes, I mean both kinds of crabs ♪ I thought that was at least gonna be innuendo.
jokes.setup: Song lyric about 'crabs' that could be sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: Character explicitly states it's NOT innuendo, it's literal
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on how crude the song actually is, but fairly predictable execution.

jokes.text: Truck races, swamp parties, mall fires.
jokes.setup: Listing cultural activities from 'the rest of the country'
jokes.punchline: Mall fires listed casually as entertainment alongside races and parties
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that treats arson as casual regional entertainment. Perfect 30 Rock absurdism with great economy.

jokes.text: Why do you have a tattoo of a seatbelt? So I don't get pulled over when I'm driving shirtless!
jokes.setup: Question about an unusual tattoo
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding of how traffic laws work - thinks visual trick will fool police
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures crabcatcher logic - creative solution to imaginary problem. High originality and perfect character work.

jokes.text: since our weekend in Aspen, she's been put on pelvic rest. Fast-forward noise.
jokes.setup: Jack oversharing about his girlfriend's medical condition
jokes.punchline: Other character makes literal 'fast-forward' noise to skip past TMI
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Jack's oversharing and creative way to show someone wants to skip uncomfortable details.

jokes.text: Fancy luncheon, seafood bar. I'm thinking 75 cents a shrimp, over three hours, and split... Carry 3.75... By the time I'm done, you'll be making money.
jokes.setup: Expensive Republican fundraiser with costly food
jokes.punchline: Liz calculates she can eat enough shrimp to make the $10,000 ticket profitable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon logic - approaching politics through food math. Shows her character perfectly.

jokes.text: Good Lord, Lemon. You just locked your mouth and then swallowed the key. It makes no earthly sense.
jokes.setup: Liz presumably does an exaggerated mime of keeping quiet
jokes.punchline: Jack pointing out the logical impossibility of her gesture
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack being literal and pedantic about Liz's dramatic gesture. Good character interplay.

jokes.text: Why couldn't she have died when that rabid dog bit her? It wasn't rabid. I just said that so they'd have to put it down, and then I'd be the star of that dog-food commercial.
jokes.setup: Tracy wishing Jenna had died from dog bite
jokes.punchline: Jenna reveals she lied about rabies to kill innocent dog for commercial opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jenna's narcissistic evil - willing to kill innocent animal for minor career gain. Dark but earned through character.

jokes.text: If I get moderate exercise, I'm gonna die!
jokes.setup: Complaining about having to walk an extra block
jokes.punchline: Claims doctors warned exercise will literally kill him
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy hypochondria and laziness. Perfect character moment but not particularly original.

jokes.text: And I'm mad at your success, but pretending it's something else!
jokes.setup: Tracy being angry at Jenna
jokes.punchline: He admits he's jealous but disguising it as other complaints
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where Tracy admits his real motivation. Self-aware and economical.

jokes.text: Please. I've had a crab on my head for free!
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to sell crab hats
jokes.punchline: Tracy implying he's had actual crabs (STD) before
jokes.explanation: Sexual innuendo that's fairly obvious but delivered with Tracy's characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: I'm lying. The claws hold your cigarette while you uncork a 'brew-skye.'
jokes.setup: Jenna immediately admits she was lying about Tracy
jokes.punchline: Describes ridiculous product features including made-up word 'brew-skye'
jokes.explanation: Jenna's casual dishonesty and the absurd product description with fake slang term.

jokes.text: 'Uncork a brew-skye'? Yeah, you know. Ka-zap. Blinky, blinky, blinky, blinky. Those aren't even the right noises.
jokes.setup: Questioning Jenna's made-up slang
jokes.punchline: Jenna makes random sound effects, gets called out for wrong noises
jokes.explanation: Jenna's fake expertise being exposed through nonsensical sound effects. Good escalation.

jokes.text: You're maybe the most high-maintenance bitch in Hollywood. Maybe? Who's more? Who is she?
jokes.setup: Tracy insulting Jenna as high-maintenance
jokes.punchline: Jenna focuses on 'maybe' - wants to know who could possibly be worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - takes insult as challenge to her supremacy. Excellent misdirection and character work.

jokes.text: You medical office before-pictures.
jokes.setup: Jenna insulting the crabcatchers
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to unflattering before photos in medical ads
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that's both specific and devastating. Good economy and originality.

jokes.text: I don't need some Kenyan-born college professor telling me I didn't build my company. Because I earned my trust fund by always being polite to grandfather.
jokes.setup: Rich Republican complaining about Obama's business comments
jokes.punchline: Claims to have 'earned' inherited money through basic politeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of inherited wealth entitlement - 'earning' a trust fund through minimal effort.

jokes.text: Reginald, I believe you're thinking of the White House.
jokes.setup: Republican calling NBC full of communists and homosexuals
jokes.punchline: Jack dryly suggests he means the Obama White House instead
jokes.explanation: Jack's smooth political redirect. Clean structure but fairly predictable political humor.

jokes.text: Michelle Obama's on steroids.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to fit in at Republican fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Blurts out random, nonsensical political attack
jokes.explanation: Liz's awkward attempt to say something Republican-friendly results in absurd claim. Good character moment.

jokes.text: No, it's different from every other camera in the world.
jokes.setup: Tourist asking how to use camera to take Jenna's photo
jokes.punchline: Jenna sarcastically explains cameras work differently from each other
jokes.explanation: Jenna's disdain for tourists through sarcasm. Functional but not particularly original.

jokes.text: You look like a condom that's been dropped on the floor of a barbershop.
jokes.setup: Jenna insulting a crabcatcher
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and gross visual comparison
jokes.explanation: Fantastically specific and disgusting insult. High originality and vivid imagery that's very quotable.

jokes.text: Will you sign my Cesarean scar?
jokes.setup: Fan asking for autograph
jokes.punchline: Wants signature on surgical scar instead of normal item
jokes.explanation: Appropriately gross fan request that escalates the weirdness. Good economy and shock value.

jokes.text: That's why I said, 'you more on.' Like, 'pour more on me!' Whoo!
jokes.setup: Jenna calling someone a moron
jokes.punchline: Crabcatcher pretends it was beach-party wordplay, not insult
jokes.explanation: Clever way to deflect an insult by repurposing it as party slang. Shows character's oblivious positivity.

jokes.text: We should go to a pumpkin patch? Pranksmen, activate.
jokes.setup: Kenneth suggesting innocent activity in response to prank planning
jokes.punchline: Frank's dramatic 'activate' command contrasts with Kenneth's wholesomeness
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast. Kenneth's innocent suggestion vs. Frank's action movie declaration.

jokes.text: Obama's bailout would never have worked if big business hadn't heroically taken it. But where's our parade?
jokes.setup: Republican discussing financial bailouts
jokes.punchline: Wants credit and parades for accepting government money
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate entitlement - wanting praise for accepting bailout money. Solid political humor.

jokes.text: You believe in the death penalty, but it's okay to kill animals for food? I don't know where I'm going with this yet. Hang on. Hang on!
jokes.setup: Liz trying to make political arguments
jokes.punchline: Admits she doesn't know her own point mid-argument
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where Liz breaks character to admit she's winging her political argument. Very relatable and honest.

jokes.text: And I hope it's gay... Male gay. Because with the ladies, it's too much hiking.
jokes.setup: Liz talking about hoping for a gay baby
jokes.punchline: Prefers male gay because lesbians do too much outdoor activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz logic - stereotyping lesbians through her own anti-exercise bias. Good character integration.

jokes.text: They make love to their husband's blowholes.
jokes.setup: Liz arguing about gay dolphins
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of dolphin homosexual activity
jokes.explanation: Crude but fits Liz's unhinged political rant. Appropriately uncomfortable for the setting.

jokes.text: If birth control pills fixed boners, you would get them free with your driver's license.
jokes.setup: Liz making point about healthcare access
jokes.punchline: Hypothetical about male-focused medication being freely available
jokes.explanation: Sharp political commentary with good structure and economy. Makes clear point about gender bias in healthcare.

jokes.text: And in her apartment there is a black-and-white photograph where you can almost see a breast. It's an original Leonard Nimoy.
jokes.setup: Jack describing Liz's liberal apartment as having scandalous art
jokes.punchline: The 'nude' photo is actually by Star Trek's Spock
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - sets up scandalous art, reveals it's by beloved sci-fi actor. Great character detail and structure.

jokes.text: You are my chum. The bait I throw in the water to attract the big fish. Damn it! Second meaning.
jokes.setup: Liz angry about being called Jack's 'chum' (friend)
jokes.punchline: Jack reveals he meant 'chum' as fish bait, not friend
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes earlier moment. Perfect double-meaning payoff with Jack's characteristic manipulation.

jokes.text: The belt is a baby's necktie.
jokes.setup: Describing Cerie wearing a shirt as a dress
jokes.punchline: The belt is actually infant formal wear
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail delivered in Kenneth's matter-of-fact way. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: All that counts is what's inside... Your blender.
jokes.setup: Jenna seeming to give sincere advice about inner beauty
jokes.punchline: Redirects to superficial concern about kitchen appliances
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows Jenna can't maintain sincerity for long. Classic character moment.

jokes.text: You... virgins, have no idea who you're messing with.
jokes.setup: Jenna threatening Kenneth and Frank
jokes.punchline: Uses 'virgins' as her go-to devastating insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna insult choice - projects her own sexual obsession as the ultimate putdown.

jokes.text: Because that's our capital.
jokes.setup: Jenna threatening they'll never set foot in Florida again
jokes.punchline: Claims Florida is the crabcatchers' capital
jokes.explanation: Jenna's geographic confusion while making threats. Shows her poor knowledge while maintaining authority.

jokes.text: The rides there capture the thrills and chills of the movies.
jokes.setup: Frank listing reasons he needs to go to Florida
jokes.punchline: Delivers cheesy theme park marketing copy completely straight
jokes.explanation: Frank's deadpan delivery of obvious tourism marketing. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Just as soon as it's cool for them to drive.
jokes.setup: Jenna threatening her crabcatcher army will come for them
jokes.punchline: Admits her followers are too hot from sun exposure to drive
jokes.explanation: Undermines Jenna's threat by acknowledging her army's physical limitations. Good callback.

jokes.text: And Ed Begley Jr. wasn't available, because the sail on his car broke.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he didn't use another liberal target
jokes.punchline: References Begley's extreme environmental activism with absurd car detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd detail about liberal environmental extremism. Good specificity and economy.

jokes.text: They want their honeys boo boo and their Sunday night feetball, which is the plural of football.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining what Americans want from TV
jokes.punchline: Makes up fake plural 'feetball' and explains it seriously
jokes.explanation: Jack's fake intellectual explanation for made-up word. Good character pomposity.

jokes.text: I guess that's why they call me Mitt. 'Baseball Mitt Romney' and 'Barack a llama' aren't saying anything.
jokes.setup: Liz commenting on their political comedy
jokes.punchline: Admits their candidate puns are meaningless
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on lazy political comedy. Self-aware but the puns themselves aren't great.

jokes.text: Like that otter that looked just like Tracy.
jokes.setup: Liz wanting content that will go viral
jokes.punchline: References viral video comparing Tracy to an otter
jokes.explanation: Random absurd detail about Tracy's resemblance to animals. Good economy but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Great-uncle? Momma's baby is out of 'marsh-mal-lows.'
jokes.setup: Lutz's supposedly hot young actor nephew visiting
jokes.punchline: Speaks like a baby, mispronounces 'marshmallows'
jokes.explanation: Reveals the 'hot actor' is actually mentally childlike. Good subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: become the 11th legitimate President of the United States.
jokes.setup: Jack talking about helping Romney become president
jokes.punchline: Claims Romney would be the 11th 'legitimate' president
jokes.explanation: Implies most US presidents were illegitimate. Good political absurdism from Jack's elitist perspective.

jokes.text: But here's the thing, Jack. My brother-dad, which is mormon for 'dad,'
jokes.setup: Romney's son explaining Mormon family structure
jokes.punchline: Made-up term for polygamous family relationships
jokes.explanation: Mild religious humor about Mormon polygamy. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: if I didn't know you were going to get your own planet when you die.
jokes.setup: Jack threatening to slap Garrett
jokes.punchline: References Mormon belief about getting planets in afterlife
jokes.explanation: Jack's deadpan reference to Mormon theology. Good delivery but expected religious humor.

jokes.text: How dare you talk that way in front of the pile.
jokes.setup: Garrett saying money can't solve racism
jokes.punchline: Jack treats pile of money like it has feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - anthropomorphizing money. Great character integration and absurdism.

jokes.text: I support Mitt Romney. Good, continue.
jokes.setup: Don Cheadle starting endorsement
jokes.punchline: Jack interrupting to encourage more content from paid actor
jokes.explanation: Shows the artificial nature of paid endorsements. Good meta moment.

jokes.text: Mitt Romney is a layup.
jokes.setup: Cheadle forced to endorse Romney
jokes.punchline: Uses basketball term inappropriately for political endorsement
jokes.explanation: Awkward sports metaphor in forced endorsement. Shows disconnect but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: What's crackin', my homies? Jazz gets down with the rom-nizzle.
jokes.setup: Transformer character endorsing Romney
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous slang and 'rom-nizzle' nickname for Romney
jokes.explanation: Appropriately cringey attempt at urban marketing. Captures the awkwardness of pandering.

jokes.text: 'Dy-no-mite.' For heaven's sakes.
jokes.setup: Presumably Jazz says the outdated catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Jack's exasperated reaction to the terrible marketing
jokes.explanation: Jack's refined sensibilities clashing with crude marketing. Good reaction beat.

jokes.text: Over there, they solve all their problems with money. They use it to put out fires. Ce feu m'ennuie.
jokes.setup: Jack praising Monaco's money-centric solutions
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about extinguishing fires with cash, plus French phrase meaning 'this fire bores me'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd wealth fantasy. The French phrase about fire being boring is perfect rich person detachment. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: How are you? Jack was right. People don't want an idea bomb dropped on them. Don't give up. That is not the Lisa Loeb I know.
jokes.setup: Tracy seemingly offering encouragement to Liz
jokes.punchline: Confuses her with singer Lisa Loeb
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy confusion moment. Sets up sincere encouragement then reveals he doesn't know who he's talking to.

jokes.text: What they don't know is that he hunts humans on that property.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining Romney's New Hampshire vacation home
jokes.punchline: Casually claims Romney hunts people for sport
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist political humor delivered matter-of-factly. Good shock value and economy.

jokes.text: I don't care if it's Obama talking about health care or me talking about white butts. They are different than black butts.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining North Carolina's resistance to lectures
jokes.punchline: Compares Obama's healthcare talks to his own racial comedy material
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - equating presidential policy with his crude comedy. Good character integration.

jokes.text: The voting machines there have become sentient. And, for some reason, they are strongly in favor of gay marriage.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why Pennsylvania goes to Obama
jokes.punchline: Machines gaining consciousness and political opinions
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist premise with specific political stance

jokes.text: The voting machines there have become sentient. And, for some reason, they are strongly in favor of gay marriage.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Pennsylvania goes to Obama
jokes.punchline: Voting machines achieved consciousness and have progressive political views
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd explanation for voting patterns. High originality and perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Ever since Tracy set fire to Lambeau Field, Wisconsinites are coming around on the death penalty.
jokes.setup: Explaining Wisconsin's political shift
jokes.punchline: References his own past arson as cause for death penalty support
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Tracy's criminal past affecting politics. Good callback and character consistency.

jokes.text: Florida, the penis of America!
jokes.setup: Tracy building to final battleground state
jokes.punchline: Describes Florida using crude anatomical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Obvious geographical joke but delivered with Tracy's enthusiasm. Sets up the Florida focus.

jokes.text: Florida, the penis of America!
jokes.setup: Tracy identifying the final swing state
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate anatomical description of Florida's shape
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective geographical observation. Becomes running gag in episode.

jokes.text: Or I will drop a 'D' in the greenroom.
jokes.setup: Jenna demanding to be fed baby food
jokes.punchline: Threatens to defecate in workplace if not accommodated
jokes.explanation: Peak Jenna diva behavior - bathroom threats for celebrity demands. Appropriately gross and entitled.

jokes.text: Something you mainlanders wouldn't understand. Pete, come on. Pete's dead. I'm Panama, now.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his transformation after hanging with crabcatchers
jokes.punchline: Declares his old identity dead, now goes by 'Panama'
jokes.explanation: Perfect midlife crisis transformation. Pete's stressed character finding fake enlightenment.

jokes.text: One week they're laughing at me, the next week, they're laughing at me.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to explain unpredictable Northern Florida audiences
jokes.punchline: Realizes his 'different' examples are actually the same
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where Tracy's contrast reveals no actual difference. Clean structure.

jokes.text: The next president of the United States will be chosen by... Jenna Maroney. To be continued... Mr. Spider. Ew, web in my mouth!
jokes.setup: Tracy building to dramatic revelation about election
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's spider nemesis interrupts the cliffhanger moment
jokes.explanation: Good cliffhanger punctured by Kenneth's bizarre personal drama. Nice tonal whiplash.

jokes.text: Ew, web in my mouth!
jokes.setup: Kenneth encountering 'Mr. Spider' mentioned in preview
jokes.punchline: Disgusted reaction to getting spiderweb in mouth
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy reaction. Kenneth's disgust is appropriately childlike.

jokes.text: Will NBC's head of promotions ever get that mousetrap off his penis?
jokes.setup: Fake 'next week' preview with various questions
jokes.punchline: Randomly mentions executive's bizarre personal injury
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random and specific injury detail thrown into preview. High shock value and originality.

tmdb_overview: Liz and Jack find themselves battling to influence voters in the upcoming presidential election. Meanwhile, as Jenna goes to great lengths to please her new fan base, the writers seek out new ways to mess with her.
insight: Absurdist density peaks at 2.79 jokes per minute across 65 rapid-fire gags.
